=== Release History ===
{{Collapsible section
| title = Release History
| collapsed = no
| content = 

;v0.0.x series (Initial releases)
{{Collapsible section
| title = 
| collapsed = yes
| content = 
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Version
! Date
! Notes
! Summary of Changes
|-
| v0.0.0
| November 27, 2025
| Initial release
| Initial prototype release: Core parser (nom), AST, resolver with type inference/trait resolution, affine borrow checker, MIR, LLVM codegen with JIT; algebraic semiring structures; actor runtime; TimingOwned constant-time guarantees; hybrid traits; defer RAII; standard library embeddings.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.0.0|title=Zeta v0.0.0|website=github.com|access-date=December 1, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.0.1
| December 4, 2025
| Pre-release
| Initial alpha release with core compiler: parser, resolver, MIR, LLVM codegen, minimal actor/std runtime.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.0.1|title=Zeta v0.0.1|website=github.com|access-date=December 7, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.0.2
| December 7, 2025
| Pre-release
| Self-hosting JIT bootstrap; affine checking; TimingOwned constant-time; type inference/generics/structural; semiring CTFE; actor M:N scheduler; LLVM/SIMD/MLGO improvements.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.0.2|title=Zeta v0.0.2|website=github.com|access-date=December 9, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.0.3
| December 13, 2025
| Pre-release
| Unified String type; affine ownership in MIR; semiring operations and CTFE; specialization cache; async with Tokio/mpsc; bootstrap JIT; generics/hybrid dispatch/enums/structs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.0.3|title=Zeta v0.0.3|website=github.com|access-date=December 15, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.0.4
| December 14, 2025
| Pre-release
| Unified str with concatenation, f-strings, rich methods; implicit borrows; zero-cost Vec<u8> interop; structural dispatch.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.0.4|title=Zeta v0.0.4|website=github.com|access-date=December 16, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.0.5
| December 15, 2025
| Pre-release
| Unified string support with rich operations; ABI/safety checks; CTFE semiring folding; affine moves; async actor runtime; bootstrap pipeline; optimizations (SIMD/MLGO).<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.0.5|title=Zeta v0.0.5|website=github.com|access-date=December 17, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.0.6
| December 17, 2025
| Pre-release
| Error propagation (? and Result); dictionary/map support; single-line functions; complex assignments; parsing/AST/resolver/borrow checker/MIR/codegen updates.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.0.6|title=Zeta v0.0.6|website=github.com|access-date=December 19, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.0.7
| December 17, 2025
| Pre-release
| Full annotations audit and documentation update; Cargo.toml bump; no breaking changes.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.0.7|title=Zeta v0.0.7|website=github.com|access-date=January 24, 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://crates.io/crates/zetac/0.0.7|title=zetac - Rust|website=crates.io|access-date=December 19, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.0.8
| December 18, 2025
| Pre-release
| Actor runtime enhancements (global channel map, reqwest HTTP GET, simulated TLS); codegen parameter init fixes; MIR error propagation improvements.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.0.8|title=Zeta v0.0.8|website=github.com|access-date=January 24, 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://crates.io/crates/zetac/0.0.8|title=zetac - Rust|website=crates.io|access-date=December 20, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.0.9
| December 19, 2025
| Pre-release
| Bug fixes in parser, code generation, and resolver; full enum support; trait resolution and generics for Result/Map; MIR implementation for generics, error propagation, DictLit/Subscripts; monomorphization extraction.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.0.9|title=Zeta v0.0.9|website=github.com|access-date=January 24, 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://crates.io/crates/zetac/0.0.9|title=zetac - Rust|website=crates.io|access-date=December 21, 2025}}</ref>
|}
}}

;v0.1.x series (Core features)
{{Collapsible section
| title = 
| collapsed = yes
| content = 
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Version
! Date
! Notes
! Summary of Changes
|-
| v0.1.0
| December 25, 2025
|
| Major refactor into modular layers (frontend/middle/backend/runtime); CacheSafe TBAA/TimingOwned; thin monomorphization; Affine checker 2.0; actor runtime with M:N threads. Comprehensive testing suite added.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.1.0|title=Zeta v0.1.0 Release Notes|website=github.com|access-date=December 27, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.1.1
| December 27, 2025
|
| Full f-string interpolation with nested expressions; defer statements with RAII lowering; internal fixes for MIR/codegen/TBAA; Rust 2024/Inkwell 7.1/LLVM 21.1/nom 8.0 compatibility. Core language features complete.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.1.1|title=Zeta v0.1.1 — The Ergonomics Release|website=github.com|access-date=December 29, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.1.2
| December 28, 2025
|
| Advanced trait resolution with associated types; parametric generics/monomorphization for Result<T>/Map<K,V>; full compile-time function evaluation (CTFE); expanded stable ABI checks with specialization cache. Semantic analysis phase complete.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.1.2|title=Zeta v0.1.2 Release Notes|website=github.com|access-date=December 30, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.1.3
| December 28, 2025
|
| Algebraic SemiringFold fusion for +/* chains; proper RAII defer lowering (LIFO cleanup); full Result<?> propagation via TryProp MIR; native dictionary literals and subscripting with host map intrinsics. MIR extensions checklist complete.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.1.3|title=Zeta v0.1.3 Released|website=github.com|access-date=December 30, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.1.4
| December 28, 2025
|
| Pure LLVM str_concat implementation (malloc + memcpy); MLGO-driven optimization pipeline with Grok-recommended passes; full SIMD vectorization of SemiringFold chains; native target configuration and aggressive optimizations. Up to 3.2× speedup on algebraic workloads and 40% smaller binaries. Codegen checklist complete.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.1.4|title=Zeta v0.1.4 Released|website=github.com|access-date=January 24, 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://crates.io/crates/zetac/0.1.4|title=zetac - Rust|website=crates.io|access-date=December 30, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.1.5
| December 29, 2025
|
| "The Optimizer": Full CTFE of binary operations, call chains, and const functions with new CTFE cache; SemiringFold fusion of multiplication chains in MIR for enhanced vectorization; persistent disk caching of monomorphized/specialized functions; codebase cleanup (Mir derives Default, all Clippy warnings resolved). Prepares for concurrency enhancements in v0.1.6.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.1.5|title=Zeta v0.1.5 "The Optimizer"|website=github.com|access-date=January 24, 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://crates.io/crates/zetac/0.1.5|title=zetac - Rust|website=crates.io|access-date=December 31, 2025}}</ref>
|-
| v0.1.6
| December 31, 2025
|
| "The Concurrency Revolution is Here!": Robust actor system with global channel registry, real HTTP GET (reqwest + rustls), single global Tokio runtime.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.1.6|title=Zeta v0.1.6 — The Concurrency Revolution is Here!|website=github.com|access-date=January 2, 2026}}</ref>
|-
| v0.1.7
| January 4, 2026
|
| "Ergonomics Revolution!": Rich string methods, + for concatenation, implicit &str conversions, visual MIR dumps, DWARF metadata.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.1.7|title=Zeta v0.1.7 - Ergonomics Revolution!|website=github.com|access-date=January 6, 2026}}</ref>
|}
}}

;v0.2.x series (Self-hosting foundation)
{{Collapsible section
| title = 
| collapsed = yes
| content = 
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Version
! Date
! Notes
! Summary of Changes
|-
| v0.2.0
| January 5, 2026
|
| "The Self-Hosting Dawn!": Core compiler components (parser, AST, borrow checker, resolver, MIR, runtime interfaces) ported to pureZeta; modular self-hosted structure.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.2.0|title=Zeta v0.2.0 - The Self-Hosting Dawn!|website=github.com|access-date=January 7, 2026}}</ref>
|-
| v0.2.1
| January 14, 2026
|
| "The Execution Breakthrough!": Real JIT execution from Zeta IR; enhanced emission for locals/calls/branches/dict ops; full host mappings.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.2.1|title=Zeta v0.2.1 - The Execution Breakthrough!|website=github.com|access-date=January 16, 2026}}</ref>
|-
| v0.2.2
| January 20, 2026
|
| "The Bootstrap Breakthrough": Full frontend (parser, AST, borrow checker), middle-end (resolver, CTFE, MIR), backend (LLVM IR emission), and runtime ported to Zeta; non-trivial programs with actors/maps/strings run natively.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.2.2|title=Zeta v0.2.2 Release Notes — The Bootstrap Breakthrough|website=github.com|access-date=January 22, 2026}}</ref>
|-
| v0.2.3
| January 20, 2026
|
| "The Execution Horizon": Real execution of Zeta-emitted LLVM IR via full Inkwell-backed JIT; IR loaded, optimized, and executed in-memory with global host function mapping.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.2.3|title=Zeta v0.2.3 Release Notes — The Execution Horizon|website=github.com|access-date=January 22, 2026}}</ref>
|}
}}

;v0.3.x series (Rust implementation maturity)
{{Collapsible section
| title = 
| collapsed = yes
| content = 
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Version
! Date
! Notes
! Summary of Changes
|-
| v0.3.0
| January 20, 2026
| 
| "The Dawn of True Self-Hosting": Full self-hosted bootstrap achieved; compiler written almost entirely in Zeta (~3,100 LOC); minimal Rust glue for runtime/JIT; affine borrow checker, MIR lowering, and semiring fusion implemented in Zeta; binary identity milestone; compiles itself in 14 ms.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.3.0|title=Zeta v0.3.0 — The Dawn of True Self-Hosting|website=github.com|access-date=January 23, 2026}}</ref>
|-
| v0.3.1–v0.3.18
| January–March 2026
| 
| Incremental improvements, bug fixes, and feature enhancements including generic type system foundation, static method support, performance optimizations, closures, pattern matching, module system, and runtime fixes.
|-
| '''v0.3.19'''
| March 29, 2026
| Latest release
| "The Integration Release": Comprehensive async/await runtime support, algebraic data types with enhanced pattern matching, macro system enhancements including attribute macro expansion, standard library integration (Option, Result, atomic operations), Rust 2024 edition compliance.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.3.19|title=Zeta v0.3.19 — The Integration Release|website=github.com|access-date=March 30, 2026}}</ref>
|}
}}

;v0.4.x series (Pure Zeta self-hosting)
{{Collapsible section
| title = 
| collapsed = yes
| content = 
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Version
! Date
! Notes
! Summary of Changes
|-
| v0.4.0
| January 29, 2026
| 
| Pure Zeta source delivered; final pure-Zeta milestone before self-host binary transition.
|-
| '''v0.4.1'''
| February 18, 2026
| 
| "Pure Zeta Self-Host from Latest Binaries": Full pure self-host pipeline using latest binaries; official build process now 100% Zeta-driven; self-host milestone documentation updated.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://github.com/murphsicles/zeta/releases/tag/v0.4.1|title=Zeta v0.4.1 — Pure Zeta Self-Host from Latest Binaries|website=github.com|access-date=February 18, 2026}}</ref>
|}
}}

;v0.5.x series (Pure Zeta development - future)
{{Collapsible section
| title = 
| collapsed = yes
| content = 
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Version
! Status
! Target features
|-
| v0.5.0
| In development
| Pure Zeta self-hosted implementation, complete generic type system with monomorphization, enhanced standard library, full v0.5.0 syntax compatibility.
|}
}}
}}
